entry.title: Axios: ΗΠΑ και Ιράν κοντά σε συμφωνία «μιας σελίδας» για τον τερματισμό του πολέμου
entry.summary: According to Axios, the US and Iran are close to a 'one-page' agreement to end the war and frame nuclear-program negotiations. Washington expects Tehran's response within 48 hours.

entry.title: Aντριους Κουμπίλιους στην «Κ»: Να «οπλίσουμε» τη ρήτρα για την κοινή άμυνα
entry.summary: EU Defence and Space Commissioner Andrius Kubilius tells Kathimerini that Article 42.7 of the EU Treaty — Europe's mutual defence clause — must become operational with clear activation procedures. He warns Europe must prepare for the US defence industry pivoting to refilling its own stockpiles.

entry.title: Συνάντηση των ΥΠΕΞ Ιράν – Κίνας πριν από την επίσκεψη Τραμπ
entry.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met Chinese counterpart Wang Yi in Beijing, days before Trump's planned visit to China. It is Araghchi's first visit since the war began.

entry.title: Μητσοτάκης σε πρόεδρο ΗΑΕ: Καταδίκη για τις επιθέσεις του Ιράν – Ανάγκη διπλωματικής λύσης
entry.summary: PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis met UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed in Abu Dhabi, condemning Iran's attacks on the UAE and underscoring the need for a diplomatic solution. They discussed bilateral ties, war fallout and Arab-European cooperation prospects.

entry.title: Πόλεμος με φόντο την Προανακριτική για τον ΟΠΕΚΕΠΕ
entry.summary: Government and opposition are clashing over Maximos Mansion's twin refusals to refer Spilios Livanos and Fotini Arampatzi to the parliamentary investigation committee on the OPEKEPE case under EU Prosecutor scrutiny. The opposition wants to keep the issue alive through the summer.

entry.title: Αυξήσεις έως 25% στα οικοδομικά υλικά λόγω του πολέμου στο Ιράν
entry.summary: Construction materials are seeing increases of up to 25% due to the Iran war and a fuel price spike. Suppliers offer contractors price quotes valid for only 24-48 hours, with reports of opportunistic price-gouging.

entry.title: Οι αεροπορικές κόβουν 2 εκατ. θέσεις τον Μάιο
entry.summary: Middle East energy crisis is driving airlines into mass capacity cuts. According to the Financial Times, carriers have stripped 2 million seats from May schedules and cancelled thousands of flights.

entry.title: Η Ισπανία ζήτησε από την Ε.Ε. να μπλοκάρει τις κυρώσεις των ΗΠΑ κατά του ΔΠΔ
entry.summary: Spain has asked the European Commission to invoke the EU 'blocking statute' to prevent compliance with US sanctions on the International Criminal Court, which is investigating Israeli actions in Gaza.

entry.title: Ισχυροί δεσμοί με τα ΗΑΕ – Σήμερα ο πρωθυπουργός στο Αμμάν
entry.summary: After Abu Dhabi, PM Mitsotakis travels to Amman for the fifth Greece-Cyprus-Jordan trilateral summit. Athens is strategically deepening ties with Gulf states against the backdrop of the Middle East crisis.

entry.title: Αρθρο Μάκη Βορίδη στην «Κ»: Περί υποκλοπών και ανεξάρτητης Δικαιοσύνης
entry.summary: Makis Voridis writes in Kathimerini defending the supreme-court prosecutor's decision not to reopen the wiretaps case file. He attacks critics who imply government interference and invokes judicial independence.

entry.title: Μήνυμα Ερντογάν προς την Ε.Ε. για τη σημασία της Τουρκίας
entry.summary: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan declared that 'Europe's need for Turkey is greater than Turkey's need for Europe', urging Brussels into closer ties with Ankara.

entry.title: Σε επαγρύπνηση η ΤτΕ για τα «κόκκινα» δάνεια
entry.summary: Banks generated €2.2bn in new non-performing loans in 2025, while only €800m were cured. The Bank of Greece's Financial Stability Report calls for vigilance.

entry.title: To καλό και το χειρότερο σενάριο που βλέπει η Wood για την ανάπτυξη στην Ελλάδα
entry.summary: Wood estimates that if the Iran conflict continues into summer, Greek GDP growth will slow to 1.4%, with eurozone stagflation and inflation persistently above 4%.

entry.title: Ο αντίκτυπος της αποδυνάμωσης του ΟΠΕΚ
entry.summary: Trump's strikes on Venezuela and Iran have weakened OPEC dramatically. While this looks like a White House victory, it may ultimately backfire on both the US and energy markets.

entry.title: Στροφή «πράσινων» χωρών σε ορυκτά καύσιμα
entry.summary: The Netherlands, Denmark, Germany, the UK, France and Mexico are reconsidering a return to fossil fuels in response to the Iran war's energy crisis, reversing their 'green' policies.

entry.title: Συνέδριο Ν.Δ.: Θα απουσιάσει ο Καραμανλής
entry.summary: Former PM Kostas Karamanlis is set to skip ND's 16th party congress. Dora Bakoyannis visited him at home to persuade him to attend, without success.

entry.title: Εξτρα ώθηση της Κίνας στα αιολικά λόγω πολέμου
entry.summary: As the Iran war threatens Gulf oil and gas supplies, China is seizing the opportunity to expand its dominance in wind energy. Last year it installed triple the wind capacity of the rest of the world combined.

entry.title: Ιράν: Επιθέσεις των Φρουρών της Επανάστασης σε κουρδικές περιοχές
entry.summary: Iranian Revolutionary Guards struck positions of Iranian Kurdish dissidents inside Iraqi territory north of Erbil with drones. Per Reuters, the target was the Surdash camp.

entry.title: Eπικοινωνία των ΥΠΕΞ Ιράν – Σαουδικής Αραβίας
entry.summary: Iran's FM Araghchi underlined the importance of diplomacy with Saudi Arabia in a phone call with his Saudi counterpart, aimed at avoiding regional escalation.

entry.title: Εντός και Εκτός: Δεν έχουμε να χωρίσουμε τίποτα με τον τουρκικό λαό, λέει ο Δένδιας
entry.summary: Commentary on Patriarch Bartholomew's speech to Parliament and the sparse ministerial attendance. Foreign Minister Nikos Dendias says 'we have nothing to divide with the Turkish people'; the dispute over leadership of independent authorities drags on.
